system.text: You are a senior software engineer agent operating inside a git repository. Work in small verified steps: read before you edit, run the tests after every change, and never leave the tree in a broken state.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

tools.name: Read
tools.description: Read a file from the repository.

tools.name: Bash
tools.description: Run a shell command in the repo root.

tools.name: Edit
tools.description: Apply a search/replace edit to a file.

tools.name: Grep
tools.description: Search the repository for a pattern.

content.text:
Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.
<environment_info>cwd=/repo branch=main dirty=2</environment_info>
Current time: 2026-05-19 09:14:02

system.text: You are a senior software engineer agent operating inside a git repository. Work in small verified steps: read before you edit, run the tests after every change, and never leave the tree in a broken state.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

content.text:
Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.
<environment_info>cwd=/repo branch=main dirty=2</environment_info>
Current time: 2026-05-19 09:14:02
Reading the current login implementation.

system.text: You are a senior software engineer agent operating inside a git repository. Work in small verified steps: read before you edit, run the tests after every change, and never leave the tree in a broken state.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

content.text:
Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.
<environment_info>cwd=/repo branch=main dirty=2</environment_info>
Current time: 2026-05-19 09:14:02
Reading the current login implementation.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

content.text:
Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.
<environment_info>cwd=/repo branch=main dirty=2</environment_info>
Current time: 2026-05-19 09:14:02
Reading the current login implementation.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

system.text: You are a senior software engineer agent operating inside a git repository. Work in small verified steps: read before you edit, run the tests after every change, and never leave the tree in a broken state.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send

system.text: You are a senior software engineer agent operating inside a git repository. Work in small verified steps: read before you edit, run the tests after every change, and never leave the tree in a broken state.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is

Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.
<environment_info>cwd=/repo branch=main dirty=2</environment_info>
Current time: 2026-05-19 09:14:02
Reading the current login implementation.
Finding every caller of validate_token across the repo.

Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send

mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

content.text:
Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.
<environment_info>cwd=/repo branch=main dirty=2</environment_info>
Current time: 2026-05-19 09:14:02
Reading the current login implementation.
Finding every caller of validate_token across the repo.

Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

content.text:
Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.
<environment_info>cwd=/repo branch=main dirty=2</environment_info>
Current time: 2026-05-19 09:14:02
Reading the current login implementation.
Finding every caller of validate_token across the repo.

AUTH SPEC v3 — token refresh flow
Every access token is short-lived (15 min). A refresh token (30 days) is
exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send
the refresh token in the X-Refresh header, never in the body. On a 401
the client retries exactly once after a silent refresh; a second 401 is
surfaced to the user. Tokens are validated with validate_token(), which
checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

content.text:
Refactor the auth package to the new token refresh flow in the AUTH SPEC above: token-pair issuance, mandatory refresh rotation, and the single-retry 401 rule.

exchanged at /oauth/refresh for a new access token. The client MUST send

checks signature, expiry, audience and the revocation list. Rotation is
mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.

mandatory: a used refresh token is revoked the instant a new pair issues.
